summary: Large decomp/matching pass on `src/melee/lb/lbcollision.c`, reaching a reported 47.99% file match for 20.96 KB. The PR replaced many raw M2C-style field accesses and untyped prototypes with typed collision structs, `Vec3`/`Mtx`/`MtxPtr` usage, `float` signatures, capsule victim loops, and address-commented header declarations. It also updated scratch metadata to mark many relevant scratches `OK` and extended `tools/scratch-tracker` with ranged listing and an `ok` command backed by `tabled` output.

key_files.role: Main decomp body; 812 additions/698 deletions across many collision functions, with typed fields, matrix/vector cleanup, and several newly implemented 100% functions.
Header/prototype cleanup; added address comments, corrected return/argument types, introduced temporary `Foo`, and exposed many `lbColl_80xxxxxx` prototypes.
Struct typing fixes: `HitVictim.x4` changed from `UNK_T` to `uint`, `HitCapsule.x70` from `int` to `f32`.
Marked many scratch entries as `OK`, including numerous lbcollision scratches and surrounding library entries.
Added ranged `list` behavior and an `ok` subcommand to mark scratch completions, with table formatting.
Lockfile updates for `tabled` and related dependency/version changes.
Small callsite/signature adjustment tied to lbcollision/header typing.
Small declaration adjustment tied to lbcollision/header typing.
Small include/callsite cleanup related to collision changes.

what_changed: Reported match status for `melee/lb/lbcollision.c` became 47.99% overall, with many functions listed as 100%, including `lbColl_80008A5C`, `lbColl_80007BCC`, `lbColl_80007ECC`, `lbColl_8000A460`, `lbColl_80009F54`, `lbColl_8000A044`, `lbColl_80008440`, `lbColl_800089B8`, `lbColl_80005BB0`, `lbColl_8000ACFC`, `lbColl_80007AFC`, `lbColl_JObjSetupMatrix`, `lbColl_800083C4`, `lbColl_80007B78`, `lbColl_80008428`, and `lbColl_80008434`.

`lbcollision.c` added `<platform.h>`, changed many local/prototype types from `f32` to `float`, and added a local static prototype for `lbColl_80006E58` with a `MtxPtr` and explicit output pointer arguments.

Raw field manipulation such as `M2C_FIELD(..., u8*, ...)` was replaced in several places with typed fields like `skip_update_pos`, `bone`, `offset`, `pos`, `a_offset`, `a_pos`, `b_offset`, `b_pos`, `tangibility`, `x43_b1`, `scl`, and `size`.

Matrix code was cleaned up around `HSD_JObj` and `MtxPtr`: paths now use `HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr`/a local `HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr_fake`, `PSMTXConcat`, `HSD_MtxInverse`, and `PSMTXMUltiVec` with typed matrix/vector variables instead of many raw casts.

Victim-list management was rewritten from repeated unrolled assignments/M2C pointer arithmetic into `ARRAY_SIZE` loops for `victims_1` and `victims_2`, notably in `lbColl_80008440`, `lbColl_CopyHitCapsule`, `lbColl_800089B8`, and `lbColl_80008A5C`.

`lbcollision.h` was reorganized with address-prefixed comments such as `/* 005BB0 */`, `/* 007BCC */`, `/* 0084FC */`, etc., and many former `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` prototypes were replaced with typed signatures.
`lbcollision.h` changed the include guard from `MELEE_LB_LBCOLLISION_H` to `GALE01_005BB0` and added a temporary `Foo` typedef used by `lbColl_8000A460`.
`types.h` refined collision struct fields: `HitVictim.x4` became `uint`, and `HitCapsule.x70` became `f32`, supporting typed victim timers and collision output radius/distance values.
`config/GALE01/scratches.txt` changed many scratch completions from percentages to `OK`, including lbcollision entries and other nearby library symbols.

`tools/scratch-tracker` gained `List { start, end }` and `Ok { start, end }` commands; the new `ok` command marks matching scratch records with `Completion::Ok`, and list output now uses `tabled` with optional address display for ranges.
smart_moves: Converting repeated manual victim array assignments to `ARRAY_SIZE` loops both improved readability and exposed the real shape of `HitCapsule` victim arrays while preserving behavior.

Replacing M2C byte/bitfield access with named struct fields (`skip_update_pos`, `x43_b1`, `tangibility`) made the collision state-machine logic much easier to reason about and reusable for future collision decomp.

Using `MtxPtr`, typed `Vec3` stack temporaries, and `HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr` reduced cast noise around transform code, which is especially valuable in collision routines where matrix shape mistakes are easy.
Adding address comments to every public `lbColl_*` prototype made `lbcollision.h` searchable by ROM address and helped align header declarations with the decomp report.
Updating `scratch-tracker` with a ranged `ok` command directly supports the workflow shown in this PR: bulk-marking scratches complete after landing a decomp/matching pass.
Marking scratch entries as `OK` rather than preserving stale percentage text keeps the scratch list aligned with merged source, avoiding rediscovery of already-landed work.
decomp_lessons: For collision files, struct typing can be as important as expression matching: once `HitResult`, `HurtCapsule`, and `HitCapsule` fields are named, many M2C casts collapse into direct assignments and clearer control flow.
When a function repeatedly updates capsule positions from bones, look for a shared `skip_update_pos` bit/field rather than duplicating byte-mask logic.
Matrix-heavy code should prefer `Mtx`, `MtxPtr`, `Vec3`, `HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr`, `PSMTXConcat`, and `HSD_MtxInverse` over raw `f32*`/`void*` casts whenever the ABI permits it.
Victim/timer arrays are good candidates for `ARRAY_SIZE` loops; this can match while replacing long unrolled blocks if the compiler emits equivalent indexed loops.
Small struct field type changes (`UNK_T` to `uint`, `int` to `f32`) can unlock many downstream expression matches and reduce artificial casts.
Header prototypes should be corrected alongside source decomp; mismatched `UNK_T`, `Mtx*`, `f32*`, and return types propagate bad casts into unrelated fighter callsites.
Temporary or weakly named types like `Foo` are acceptable evidence of incomplete struct understanding, but should be revisited once adjacent data structures are better known.
`NOT_IMPLEMENTED` left in `lbColl_80008248` means this PR is not a complete lbcollision decomp; future work should inspect that function and the still-0% report entries before assuming coverage.
naming_conventions: Public library collision functions keep address-based names in the form `lbColl_800xxxxx` when semantic names are not known.
Known semantic names are preserved where available, e.g. `lbColl_CopyHitCapsule` and `lbColl_JObjSetupMatrix`.
Header declarations use ROM-offset comments such as `/* 007BCC */` before prototypes, improving address-based searchability.
Typed collision parameters in improved code use short semantic names like `hit`, `hurt`, `shield_hit`, `hit_scl_y`, `hurt_scl_y`, `hurt_pos_z`, and `pos_z` instead of only `arg0`/`arg1`.
Boolean/bitfield-style struct names such as `skip_update_pos`, `x43_b1`, and `x43_b2` are preferred over repeated byte-mask `M2C_FIELD` expressions when the field meaning is known or partially known.
Scratch completion status can be represented as `OK` in `config/GALE01/scratches.txt` once merged/matched, rather than as stale percentage values.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed many `f32` declarations to `float`; in this codebase that appears to be used to influence exact generated C and reduce cast churn while preserving 32-bit float behavior.
Introduced local inline wrappers `HSD_JObjGetMtx_fake` and `HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr_fake` that assert and call `lbColl_JObjSetupMatrix`, suggesting an inline/ABI matching tactic for JObj matrix access.
Replaced raw stack scalar groups with `Vec3` and `Mtx` locals to line up with matrix/vector helper calls and simplify stack layout reasoning.

Used named constants from `.sdata2` such as `lbColl_804D7A34`, `lbColl_804D7A38`, and `lbColl_804D7A3C` instead of literal `20.0f`, `3.0f`, or `0.5f` in some paths, likely helping data-reference matching.
Typed global data blobs as `u8[4]` and accessed byte 3 directly, replacing M2C access to `lbColl_804D36CC`, `lbColl_804D36D0`, and related collision color/descriptor data.

Some attempted cleanup was intentionally not completed: `lbColl_80008248` was changed to `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` with the previous call sequence commented, so future matching should not treat that as resolved.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or formal reviews were present in the captured PR slice, so there is no human reviewer feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Inspect later PRs touching `lbColl_80008248` to see how the `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` placeholder was resolved.
Search for later renames of temporary `Foo` in `lbcollision.h` and `lbColl_8000A460` to find the real struct type.
Review subsequent changes to `HitCapsule.x70`, `HitVictim.x4`, `skip_update_pos`, `x43_b1`, and `x43_b2` for confirmed semantics.
Look for PRs that finish the 0% report entries `lbColl_800096B4`, `lbColl_80008FC8`, `lbColl_80008DA4`, `lbColl_8000A78C`, `lbColl_8000AB2C`, `lbColl_80008820`, and `lbColl_80009DD4`.
Search scratch-tracker history for the `ok` command to see whether bulk `OK` scratch marking became the standard workflow.
